verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein with its N-terminal luminal/GOLD domain facing the extracellular/lumenal space. Its dominant steady-state localization is the ER-Golgi/ERGIC endomembrane system, where it cycles as a cargo receptor. However, multiple independent lines of evidence document bona fide plasma membrane localization: (1) the NCBI/RefSeq annotation explicitly states it is 'localized to the plasma membrane and Golgi cisternae'; (2) published studies (Blum & Lepier 2008) show the luminal domain drives surface trafficking, partially overriding the KKXX ER-retention motif, placing TMED10 in PM complexes; (3) TMED10 localizes to the PM independently of COPI; (4) its extracellular region (Thr91-Glu110) engages TGF-β receptor complexes at the cell surface. PM presence is documented but represents a minority pool cycling through the secretory pathway, qualifying as dual_localization contextual rather than stable constitutive surface.
